name: Route from Sobrepeña to Lorilla (PR-S 38) — circular through the villages of Valderredible
name_original: Ruta de Sobrepeña a Lorilla (PR-S 38) — circular por los pueblos de Valderredible
region_intro: Hiking around Valderredible offers diverse landscapes characterized by the Ebro river, dense forests like Monte Hijedo, and rolling hills. The region features a mix of river valleys, wooded areas, and open terrain, providing varied hiking experiences. Trails often follow natural watercourses and traverse historical sites. This area is suitable for exploring the natural and cultural heritage of Valderredible on foot. Best hiking trails around Valderredible The most popular hiking route is Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route explores the dramatic Ebro Canyon and includes a loop around Orbaneja del Castillo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30), a moderate 11.0 miles (17.6 km) path. This trail leads through the ancient Monte Hijedo forest, offering a journey through a significant natural woodland. Local hikers also love the Wolves' Well - Valcabado Loop, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Valderredible is defined by river valleys, ancient forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Valderredible are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Valderredible's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valderredible?
A: Valderredible offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 80 routes available. These range in difficulty from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Valderredible?
A: Yes, Valderredible has approximately 19 easy hiking trails, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These routes typically feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Valderredible?
A: Many of the easier trails in Valderredible are suitable for families. These routes often pass through scenic areas and are not overly strenuous, making them ideal for children. Consider routes with points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996941997/cascada-del-tobazo-circular-by-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Tobazo – circular by the Ebro river&lt;/a&gt; for a pleasant outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Valderredible?
A: Yes, Valderredible features several circular hiking routes, which are popular for their convenience as they start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046039038/ebro-canyon-trail-orbaneja-del-castillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Canyon Trail: Orbaneja del Castillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068357183/wolves-well-valcabado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wolves' Well - Valcabado Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or interesting water features to see on hikes in Valderredible?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its natural water features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, a striking blue spring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996941997/cascada-del-tobazo-circular-by-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Tobazo – circular by the Ebro river&lt;/a&gt; route also leads to a waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I explore while hiking in Valderredible?
A: Beyond waterfalls, Valderredible offers diverse natural attractions. Hikers can discover the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996897215/monte-hijedo-trail-prc-bu-30" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Hijedo forest&lt;/a&gt;, explore caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cucarada Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic Ebro Canyon is also a significant natural landmark.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valderredible?
A: The hiking trails in Valderredible are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 240 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to ancient forests, and the well-maintained paths that allow for exploration of the region's natural and cultural heritage.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Valderredible?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many villages and popular starting points for hikes in Valderredible offer designated parking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for information on parking facilities near the starting point, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Good place to spend the night&lt;/a&gt; highlight which indicates a parking spot.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Valderredible?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on hiking trails in Valderredible, especially in natural areas. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or areas with livestock, and to respect local regulations and wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valderredible?
A: The best time for hiking in Valderredible is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer temperatures.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to see while hiking in Valderredible?
A: Yes, Valderredible is rich in historical and cultural heritage. Many trails pass by ancient sites, including rock-cut churches and hermitages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4181747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Church of the Virgin of Carmen&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building that can be explored.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Valderredible's diverse ecosystems, including dense forests and river valleys, support a variety of wildlife. Hikers might spot deer, wild boar, various bird species, and smaller mammals. The ancient Monte Hijedo forest is particularly known for its biodiversity. Always observe wildlife from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

Small Cantabrian town in the municipality of Valderredible. It is recommendable to walk through the church of San Marcos dating from the 13th century, in which we can perceive its Romanesque style in the carving of the capitals.
Depopulated from Castilla la Vieja since the 70s. It is located on the border between Palencia and Cantabria with beautiful views of the Valderredible valley.
